Name: Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics (CfA)
Relevance: Leading research institute combining Harvard and Smithsonian resources to study the universe's origin and evolution.

Name: Harvard Undergraduate Space Initiative (HUSI)
Relevance: Primary undergraduate hub for aerospace projects, satellite design, and space policy initiatives.

Name: Harvard Rockets
Relevance: Student-led organization focused on high-power rocketry and experimental propulsion system development.

Name: Harvard Satellite Team
Relevance: Engineering group developing CubeSats and testing small satellite hardware and communication protocols.

Name: Black Hole Initiative (BHI)
Relevance: Interdisciplinary center dedicated to the study of black holes through theory, observation, and engineering.

Name: Institute for Theory and Computation (ITC)
Relevance: Research center focused on theoretical astrophysics and computational modeling of cosmic structures.

Name: Harvard Origins of Life Initiative
Relevance: Multi-departmental initiative investigating the chemical and physical processes leading to life on Earth and other planets.

Name: Department of Astronomy
Relevance: Academic department managing research in stellar evolution, cosmology, and planetary systems.

Name: Harvard Microrobotics Lab
Relevance: Research into bio-inspired robots that are frequently studied for potential extraterrestrial or confined space exploration.

Name: Harvard Biorobotics Lab
Relevance: Develops robotic systems and sensors that intersect with extreme environment exploration and teleoperation.

Name: Harvard Atmospheric Chemistry Modeling Group
Relevance: Utilizes satellite observations and remote sensing data to model atmospheric dynamics and chemical composition.

Name: Environmental Chemistry and Geochemical Group (ECGG)
Relevance: Conducts research on planetary geomorphology and environmental conditions in the solar system.

Name: Harvard University Radio Club (W1AF)
Relevance: Focuses on amateur radio communications, including satellite tracking and data reception from orbiting spacecraft.

Name: Harvard SEDS
Relevance: Harvard chapter of Students for the Exploration and Development of Space focusing on networking and competitions.

Name: Harvard Mars Project
Relevance: Inactive student research group that previously focused on Martian colonization and life support technologies.

Name: Harvard-MIT Program in Health Sciences and Technology (HST)
Relevance: Joint program investigating bioastronautics and the effects of long-duration spaceflight on human physiology.

Name: Harvard Robotics Club
Relevance: Undergraduate group building automated systems, including rovers and drone platforms relevant to space navigation.

Name: Harvard Graduate Student Space Club
Relevance: Organizes professional development and social events for graduate students interested in the space industry.

Name: Solar & Stellar X-ray Group (SSXG)
Relevance: Researches stellar coronae and high-energy processes using space-based X-ray observatories like Chandra.

Name: High-Energy Astrophysics Division (HEAD)
Relevance: Division of CfA specializing in cosmic rays, gamma rays, and the instrumentation required to detect them in space.

Name: The Galileo Project
Relevance: Research initiative aimed at identifying extraterrestrial technological signatures and anomalous interstellar objects.

Name: Center for Integrated Quantum Materials (CIQM)
Relevance: Develops quantum sensors and materials critical for advanced navigation and space-based measurement systems.

Name: Harvard Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems (MEMS) Lab
Relevance: Researches micro-propulsion and sensor technologies utilized in CubeSats and small-scale spacecraft.

Name: Harvard College Observatory (HCO)
Relevance: Historical institution managing telescope facilities and astronomical data archives.

Name: Central Engineering at CfA
Relevance: Internal engineering team providing specialized design and fabrication for space-based instrumentation.

Name: Harvard Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle Group
Relevance: Focuses on flight controls, autonomous navigation, and avionics for airborne platforms.

Name: Harvard Planetary Sciences (EPS)
Relevance: Academic track within Earth and Planetary Sciences focused on solar system dynamics and planetary geology.

Name: Harvard Radiation Laboratory
Relevance: Former wartime laboratory that contributed heavily to microwave and radar technology used in modern aerospace.

Name: Harvard-Smithsonian Radio Astronomy Service (HSRAS)
Relevance: Operates radio telescopes and analyzes cosmic microwave background and other radio signals from space.

Name: Star-Planet Connection Group
Relevance: Research group at CfA investigating interactions between exoplanets and their host stars.
